questions.question: How does the chalk grinding activity support the particulate nature of matter?
questions.answer: Grinding chalk shows that matter can be broken into smaller particles without changing its composition.

questions.question: Why does sugar dissolve in water without increasing the volume significantly?
questions.answer: Because sugar particles occupy the spaces between water particles.

questions.question: What conclusion about particle motion can be drawn from diffusion experiments?
questions.answer: Particles of matter are in continuous motion.

questions.question: Why do solids maintain a definite shape?
questions.answer: Because particles in solids are strongly attracted and fixed in position.

questions.question: Why do liquids have definite volume but no fixed shape?
questions.answer: Because liquid particles are close together but can move around each other.

questions.question: Why do gases expand to fill any container?
questions.answer: Because gas particles move freely in all directions and occupy available space.

questions.question: Explain why gases can be compressed more easily than solids.
questions.answer: Because gases have large spaces between particles whereas solids have tightly packed particles.

questions.question: What scientific principle is demonstrated by the syringe experiment?
questions.answer: It demonstrates that gases are compressible due to large spaces between particles.

questions.question: Why does diffusion occur faster in gases than in liquids?
questions.answer: Because gas particles move faster and are farther apart.

questions.question: How does temperature affect particle movement?
questions.answer: Increasing temperature increases the kinetic energy and speed of particles.

questions.question: Why does the smell of perfume spread throughout a room?
questions.answer: Because perfume particles diffuse through air due to their continuous motion.

questions.question: Why does potassium permanganate spread uniformly in water without stirring?
questions.answer: Because particles move randomly and diffuse throughout the liquid.

questions.question: What happens to particle motion when a solid melts?
questions.answer: Particles gain energy and move more freely.

questions.question: Why is melting considered a physical change?
questions.answer: Because only the physical state changes while the substance remains the same.

questions.question: Why does evaporation occur at temperatures below boiling point?
questions.answer: Because some surface particles gain enough energy to escape as vapor.

questions.question: How does wind speed affect evaporation?
questions.answer: Higher wind speed removes vapor from the surface and increases evaporation.

questions.question: Why does increasing surface area increase evaporation?
questions.answer: Because more particles are exposed at the surface and can escape as vapor.

questions.question: Why do gases have very low density compared to solids?
questions.answer: Because gas particles are far apart with large empty spaces.

questions.question: What happens to interparticle attraction when matter changes from solid to gas?
questions.answer: The interparticle attraction becomes very weak.

questions.question: Why are liquids called fluids?
questions.answer: Because their particles can move freely, allowing them to flow.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the highest kinetic energy of particles?
questions.answer: Gases have the highest kinetic energy.

questions.question: Why do solids generally have high density?
questions.answer: Because particles are tightly packed with very little space between them.

questions.question: What property of gases causes them to exert pressure on container walls?
questions.answer: Continuous movement and collisions of gas particles with container walls.

questions.question: How does particle spacing change from solid to liquid to gas?
questions.answer: Particle spacing increases progressively from solid to liquid to gas.

questions.question: State the main idea of the particle model of matter.
questions.answer: Matter consists of tiny particles that are in constant motion and have spaces and forces between them.
